signals.name: Service cycle delay is visible in live flow
signals.description: The service cycle is stretching beyond the venue's intended rhythm, creating visible lag between order, production, handoff, and guest delivery.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: service or job cycle delay expressed through restaurant service flow
evidence_types: guests wait materially longer than the house rhythm suggests
orders or requests sit too long between steps
the live cycle stalls even when demand is not extreme

signals.name: Ownership is confused during live flow
signals.description: The team hesitates, re-asks, or duplicates effort because ownership of the next move is not obvious in the moment.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: ownership confusion during flow expressed through restaurant live service
evidence_types: team members ask who owns the next step
two people assume the same work or no one assumes it
flow pauses while ownership is clarified

signals.name: Complaint is handled defensively
signals.description: A guest complaint is met with explanation, blame-shifting, or resistance instead of calm acknowledgment and structured recovery.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: complaint handled defensively expressed through restaurant guest recovery behavior
evidence_types: staff explain the problem instead of owning the recovery
guest feedback is met with friction or defensiveness
recovery language escalates tension instead of stabilizing it

signals.name: Manager is absent from the critical moment
signals.description: A live pressure point arrives and the person expected to stabilize it is not visibly present, directing, or deciding.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: manager absent from critical moment expressed through restaurant peak pressure leadership
evidence_types: rush or escalation happens without visible manager intervention
the floor looks leaderless during a pressure moment
decisions wait because the manager is not present
adapter_aliases: critical rush with no manager presence

signals.name: Equipment or asset failure breaks the flow
signals.description: A piece of equipment, workstation, or physical asset interrupts the operating rhythm enough to change timing, routing, or output quality.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: equipment or asset breaks flow expressed through restaurant live operations
evidence_types: equipment failure forces a routing or pacing change
station output drops because an asset is unreliable
service timing shifts around a broken or degraded asset

signals.name: Workaround has become normal
signals.description: The team has normalized a workaround as part of everyday operation instead of treating it as temporary friction to remove.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: workaround has become normal expressed through restaurant operating habits
evidence_types: staff describe a workaround as just how the venue works
temporary fixes have become the stable process
known friction is absorbed culturally instead of corrected
adapter_aliases: temporary fix is now the process

signals.name: Waste, loss, or leakage is unexplained
signals.description: The venue is seeing avoidable loss, comp, or waste movement without a clean explanation, containment rule, or review cadence.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: waste, loss, or leakage unexplained expressed through restaurant commercial controls
evidence_types: waste or shrink increases without explanation
void or comp movement is visible but not understood
loss shows up without review or ownership

signal_failure_map.source_ref: visible service-cycle delay in restaurant flow usually reveals live coordination and pacing breakdowns in the guest-facing cycle
cycle delay frequently shows up when bar or beverage throughput is the hidden pacing constraint
unclear ownership during live service is the clearest operating signature of blurred role ownership
ownership confusion often rides on weak handoffs and pass communication under pressure
defensive complaint handling is a direct expression of guest recovery breakdown
when recovery turns defensive, weak manager intervention often allows the moment to deteriorate
absence from a critical operating moment is the clearest live signal of a manager presence gap
a manager who is absent in the moment often also failed to pre-brief and frame the shift for that pressure window
when an asset failure visibly disrupts service rhythm, equipment reliability is the direct failure mode
asset failure also creates day-to-day tooling friction that the team starts absorbing operationally
a workaround becoming the normal process means tooling or system friction has been culturally absorbed
when workarounds normalize, control routines are also failing to escalate and remove them
unexplained waste or leakage is a direct signal that commercial control loops are not holding
loss without explanation often sits on top of weak stock and count accuracy as well as weak review
